extraction.one_line_summary: FUGLSANG attacked with MARTIN Dan on Plateau de Solaison, dropped his teammate on the climb, won by 10 seconds over PORTE who time-trialed solo to defend yellow but lost GC by 4 seconds.
tactical_patterns: FUGLSANG used MARTIN Dan as launch pad before dropping him, converting teammate attack into personal GC gain—team coordination masked individual ambition.
PORTE isolated after Col de Colombier when ARU and VALVERDE forced early selection; BMC unable to defend on first climb exposed yellow jersey.
FROOME attacked PORTE on final meters of Colombier then bridged alone on descent, demonstrating superior descending when climbing parity existed.
Stage winner bonus (10 seconds) proved decisive margin in GC, transforming what would be GC tie into FUGLSANG victory—time bonuses matter on summit finishes.

rider_signals.FUGLSANG Jakob: positive: dropped climbing specialist MARTIN Dan on sustained 9% gradient after using him as bridge, then held 1:15 gap solo for stage win and GC
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: negative: lost all teammates on Col de Colombier, couldn't follow ARU/VALVERDE forcing, then dropped by FROOME on climb finish despite time-trialing well solo on final climb
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: positive: attacked PORTE precisely at Colombier summit when rival weakest, bridged 1:15 gap alone via descent/valley to reach leaders—tactical precision and descending strength
rider_signals.ARU Fabio: positive: initial attack with VALVERDE on Colombier immediately shelled PORTE's team, forcing yellow jersey to chase without support—smart early aggression
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: neutral: attacked to set up FUGLSANG but dropped on sustained 9% Solaison climb, finished third only 1 second ahead of FROOME—strong but not elite climbing
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: combined with ARU to apply early pressure on Colombier, eliminating PORTE's team protection before main difficulty—veteran tactical awareness
